action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 14 training failed because train.py incorrectly flagged NaN in Y (labels) as error, though NaN labels are expected for missing annotations. Preprocessing succeeded and saved clean X. Analysis: Fix the NaN check to only check X for NaN; Y naturally contains NaN and will be masked in loss. After fix, training on full 350k graphs with FeatureMLP should complete and produce a checkpoint. Decision: Update train.py to correct NaN check, add gradient clipping, normalize features inside training (mean/std from preprocessing), train for 20 epochs with hidden_dim=512, batch_size=512 on GPU, then submit eval to finally get a baseline AP and measure per-epoch time.
